pi_items.source: task.json description (line 6): 'Set the spatial calibration (Analyze > Set Scale): distance=1, known=0.16, unit=micron (0.16 µm/pixel)', export_result.sh line 100 comment: 'A 10 micron scale bar at 0.16 um/px is ~62 pixels wide'
task.json description (line 6): 'Burn in a scale bar (Tools > Scale Bar): 10 µm width, White color, located in the Lower Right'
task.json description (line 6): 'Change the LUT of the Red channel (Channel 1) to Magenta', verifier.py (line 65) checks for magenta remapping
task.json description (line 6): 'Ensure the Green channel (Channel 2) remains Green', verifier.py (line 76-79) checks for green channel preservation

privileged_info_summary: The task requires users to create an accessible scientific figure using ImageJ/Fiji's built-in Fluorescent Cells sample. The output must be saved as a PNG to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/accessible_figure.png. The spatial calibration is set to 0.16 microns per pixel, and a 10-micrometer white scale bar must be added to the lower right corner. Channel 1 (red) must be remapped to Magenta using LUT, while Channel 2 remains Green. These settings ensure colorblind-accessible publication-quality figures. The verifier checks for file creation, presence of magenta and green colors, suppression of pure red pixels, and detection of white scale bar pixels (>50 pixels in the bottom-right quadrant).
